purpose.scorer_notes: The activity setup was assembled during the session rather than pre-staged before children arrived. Tables were carried in, blankets laid down, and papers distributed by the teacher in real time. By the end, the colouring purpose is evident from children in action, but the arrangement alone — prior to adult instruction and material distribution — would not have communicated the activity to an arriving child. This warrants 0.5 rather than 0 because the materials (tables, paper, colouring tools) do ultimately communicate a recognisable colouring activity once assembled, even if the staging process required adult direction.

However, the activity setup visibly requires adult handling of wooden tables and chairs that children cannot manage alone by design. The session ID references 'toy design colouring' but the activity notes say individual colouring sheets — the actual activity materials (paper, crayons) are self-serviceable, but the furniture setup is not, yielding a mixed design score of 0.5.
